# Email Connector - Full Documentation

# Set Parsing Text File Attachment as Body Message

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

When Anypoint Connector for Email (Email Connector) receives a message without a body and only an
attachment file with Content-Type as `text`, the attachment file is not processed as a separate
file. Instead, the file is included in the message body as plain text. Mule apps can use this
behavior to receive the data in the message body instead of as an attachment file.

To change the behavior of parsing attached text files when the file is the only content in the
message, set the system property `parsing.text.attachment.as.body` to either of these values:

- `true` (default)  
  Preserves the previous behavior and converts the text file attachment in the body message.
- `false`  
  Adds the attachment text file to the attachment list in the payload.

To learn how to set system properties in Anypoint Studio or for on-premises deployments, refer to
the [system properties](../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-app-properties-system) documentation.

## Jakarta Mail System Properties

Email Connector uses the Jakarta Mail library internally. Thereby, you can change certain aspects of
connector behavior by changing specific Jakarta Mail system properties. Refer to the
[troubleshoot Jakarta Mail system properties](email-troubleshooting#troubleshoot-jakarta-mail-system-properties)
section that covers some of the most used Jakarta Mail system properties.

For detailed information on these system properties, refer to the Jakarta Mail documentation.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Email Connector Examples](email-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Count Messages with Email Connector

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Email (Email Connector) provides the **Count messages - IMAP** and **Count
messages - POP3** operations, which get the total amount of messages in a specified mailbox folder.

## Configure Count Messages - IMAP Operation

The following example shows how to configure the **Count messages - IMAP** operation:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  Drag an HTTP **Listener** source to the canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector
    configuration** field to configure a global element for the operation.
4.  Configure the required HTTP **Listener** connection fields and click **OK**.
5.  Set **Path** to `/count`.
6.  Drag the **Count messages - IMAP** operation to the right of HTTP **Listener**.
7.  On the **Count messages - IMAP** configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to configure a global element for the operation.
8.  Set the following fields:
    - **Host**: `192.168.0.1`
    - **User**: `usertest`
    - **Host**: `userpass`

9.  Click **OK**.
10. For **Mailbox folder**, click **fx** to switch to literal mode and set it to
    `vars.mailboxFolder`.
11. Set **Count filter** to any of the following options:
    - **ALL (Default)**
    - **DELETED**
    - **NEW**
    - **UNREAD**

![Count messages - IMAP operation configuration with Count filter field set to NEW](_images/email-count-messages-imap.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the `email:count-messages-imap` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns:email="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email/current/mule-email.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd">
	<email:imap-config name="Email_IMAP"  >
		<email:imap-connection host="192.168.0.1" user="usertest" password="userpass" />
	</email:imap-config>
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<flow name="email1Flow"  >
		<http:listener config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/count"/>
		<email:count-messages-imap doc:name="Count messages - IMAP" config-ref="Email_IMAP" mailboxFolder="#[vars.mailboxFolder]" countFilter="NEW"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Configure Count Messages - POP3 Operation

The following example shows how to configure the **Count messages - POP3** operation:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  Drag an HTTP **Listener** source to the canvas.
3.  On the **Listener** configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector
    configuration** field to configure a global element for the operation.
4.  Configure the required HTTP **Listener** connection fields and click **OK**.
5.  Set **Path** to `/count`.
6.  Drag the **Count messages - POP3** operation to the right of HTTP **Listener**.
7.  On the **Count messages - POP3** configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to configure a global element for the operation.
8.  Set the following fields:
    - **Host**: `192.168.0.1`
    - **User**: `usertest`
    - **Host**: `userpass`

![Count messages - POP3 operation configuration](_images/email-count-messages-pop3.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the `email:count-messages-pop3` configuration looks like this:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:db="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/db" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns:email="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email/current/mule-email.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/db http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/db/current/mule-db.xsd">

	<http:listener-config >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<email:pop3-config name="Email_POP3" >
		<email:pop3-connection host="192.168.0.1" user="usertest" password="userpass" />
	</email:pop3-config>
	<flow name="email1Flow" >
		<http:listener config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/count"/>
		<email:count-messages-pop3 doc:name="Count messages - POP3" config-ref="Email_POP3"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Email Connector Examples](email-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Email Connector Reference 1.8

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Support Category:
[Select](https://www.salesforce.com/en-us/wp-content/uploads/sites/4/documents/legal/Agreements/versioning-back-support-policy.pdf)

Anypoint Connector for Email (Email Connector ) is used to list and send emails and perform
operations in different mailboxes, such as `delete` and `mark as read`.

Email Connector supports the SMTP, SMTPS, IMAP, IMAPS, POP3, and POP3s protocols.

## Configurations

---

### IMAP

Configuration for operations that are performed through the IMAP (Internet Message Access Protocol)
protocol.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#imap_imap">IMAP Connection</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#imap_imaps">IMAPS Connection</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attachment Naming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NAME</p></li><li><p>NAME_HEADERS</p></li><li><p>NAME_HEADERS_SUBJECT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how attachment names should be retrieved.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NAME</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Eagerly Fetch Content</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates whether the retrieved emails should be opened and read. The default value is "true".</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### IMAP Connection

A ConnectionProvider that returns instances of imap based MailboxConnections.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An additional custom set of properties to configure the connection session.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A TimeUnit which qualifies the #connectionTimeout, #writeTimeout and #readTimeout attributes. Defaults to SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket connection timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket read timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Write Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket write timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Host name of the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to connect with the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username password to connect with the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The port number of the mail server. '993' by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>993</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pooling Profile</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PoolingProfile">Pooling Profile</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Characteristics of the connection pool</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### IMAPS Connection

A ConnectionProvider that returns instances of imaps (secure) based MailboxConnections. The returned
connection is secured by TLS.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An additional custom set of properties to configure the connection session.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A TimeUnit which qualifies the #connectionTimeout, #writeTimeout and #readTimeout attributes. Defaults to SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket connection timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket read timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Write Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket write timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Host name of the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to connect with the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username password to connect with the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The port number of the mail server. '993' by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>993</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>TLS Configuration for the secure connection of the IMAPS protocol</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pooling Profile</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PoolingProfile">Pooling Profile</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Characteristics of the connection pool</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [Count messages - IMAP](#countMessagesImap)
- [Delete](#delete)
- [Expunge Folder](#expungeFolder)
- [List - IMAP](#listImap)
- [Mark As Deleted](#markAsDeleted)
- [Mark As Read](#markAsRead)
- [Move To Folder](#moveToFolder)

## Associated Sources

- [On New Email - IMAP](#listener-imap)

---

### POP3

Configuration for operations that are performed through the POP3 (Post Office Protocol 3) protocol.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#pop3_pop3">POP3 Connection</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#pop3_pop3s">POP3S Connection</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attachment Naming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NAME</p></li><li><p>NAME_HEADERS</p></li><li><p>NAME_HEADERS_SUBJECT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how attachment names should be retrieved.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>NAME</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### POP3 Connection

A ConnectionProvider that returns instances of pop3 based MailboxConnections.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An additional custom set of properties to configure the connection session.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A TimeUnit which qualifies the #connectionTimeout, #writeTimeout and #readTimeout attributes. Defaults to SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket connection timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket read timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Write Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket write timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Host name of the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to connect with the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username password to connect with the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The port number of the mail server. '110' by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>110</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pooling Profile</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PoolingProfile">Pooling Profile</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Characteristics of the connection pool</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### POP3S Connection

A ConnectionProvider that returns instances of pop3s (secured) based MailboxConnections. The
returned connection is secured by TLS.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An additional custom set of properties to configure the connection session.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A TimeUnit which qualifies the #connectionTimeout, #writeTimeout and #readTimeout attributes. Defaults to SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket connection timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket read timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Write Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket write timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Host name of the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to connect with the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username password to connect with the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The port number of the mail server. '995' by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>995</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>TLS Configuration for the secure connection of the POP3S protocol</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pooling Profile</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PoolingProfile">Pooling Profile</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Characteristics of the connection pool</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [Count messages - POP3](#countMessagesPop3)
- [List - POP3](#listPop3)

## Associated Sources

- [On New Email - POP3](#listener-pop3)

---

### SMTP

Configuration for operations that are performed through the SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol)
protocol.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#smtp_smtp">SMTP Connection</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#smtp_smtps">SMTPS Connection</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The "From" sender address. The person that is going to send the messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Default character encoding to use in all the messages. If not specified, the default charset in the mule configuration will be used</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default Content Transfer Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Expiration Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### SMTP Connection

A ConnectionProvider that returns instances of smtp based SenderConnections.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An additional custom set of properties to configure the connection session.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A TimeUnit which qualifies the #connectionTimeout, #writeTimeout and #readTimeout attributes. Defaults to SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket connection timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket read timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Write Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket write timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Host name of the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to connect with the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username password to connect with the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The port number of the mail server. '25' by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>25</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pooling Profile</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PoolingProfile">Pooling Profile</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Characteristics of the connection pool</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### SMTPS Connection

A ConnectionProvider that returns instances of smtps based MailboxConnections. The returned
connection is secured by TLS.

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An additional custom set of properties to configure the connection session.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timeout Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A TimeUnit which qualifies the #connectionTimeout, #writeTimeout and #readTimeout attributes. Defaults to SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>SECONDS</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket connection timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Read Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket read timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Write Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The socket write timeout value. This attribute works in tandem with #timeoutUnit. Defaults to 5</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>5</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Host name of the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>User</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username used to connect with the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username password to connect with the mail server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The port number of the mail server. '465' by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>465</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>TLS Configuration for the secure connection of the SMTPS protocol</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pooling Profile</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#PoolingProfile">Pooling Profile</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Characteristics of the connection pool</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [Send](#send)

## Operations

### Count messages - IMAP

`<email:count-messages-imap>`

Counts the emails in the mailboxFolder.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mailbox Folder</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mailbox folder where the emails are.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INBOX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count Filter</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALL</p></li><li><p>DELETED</p></li><li><p>NEW</p></li><li><p>UNREAD</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Count only messages specified with this option.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ALL</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [IMAP](#imap)

#### Throws

- EMAIL:EMAIL_COUNT_MESSAGES
- EMAIL:ACCESSING_FOLDER
- EMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- EMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Delete

`<email:delete>`

Eliminates from the mailbox the email with ID emailId. For IMAP mailboxes all the messages scheduled
for deletion (marked as DELETED) will also be erased from the folder.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mailbox Folder</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mailbox folder where the emails are going to delete</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INBOX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Email ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email ID Number of the email to delete.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [IMAP](#imap)

#### Throws

- EMAIL:EMAIL_NOT_FOUND
- EMAIL:ACCESSING_FOLDER
- EMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- EMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Expunge Folder

`<email:expunge-folder>`

Eliminates from the mailbox all the messages scheduled for deletion with the DELETED flag set.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mailbox Folder</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mailbox folder where the emails with the 'DELETED' flag are going to be scheduled to be definitely deleted</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INBOX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [IMAP](#imap)

#### Throws

- EMAIL:ACCESSING_FOLDER
- EMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- EMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### List - IMAP

`<email:list-imap>`

List all the emails (with pagination) in the configured imap mailBoxFolder that match with the
specified imapMatcher criteria.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mailbox Folder</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mailbox folder where the emails are going to be fetched</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INBOX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Match with</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#imap-matcher">Imap Matcher</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email Matcher which gives the capability of filter the retrieved emails</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delete After Retrieve</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies if the returned emails must be deleted after being retrieved or not.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Page Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size of the page used by the PagingProvider implementation for fetching the emails from the IMAP server</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>10</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pagination Offset</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size of the pagination offset used by the PagingProvider implementation for fetching the emails from the IMAP server</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Limit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of emails retrieved by the operation. Take into account that this limit only applies to the emails effectively retrieved by the operation (the ones which matched the IMAPEmailPredicateBuilder criteria) and doesnât imply any restriction over the amount of emails being retrieved from the mailbox server.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>-1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attachment Naming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NAME</p></li><li><p>NAME_HEADERS</p></li><li><p>NAME_HEADERS_SUBJECT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how attachment names should be retrieved.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of [<a href="#StoredEmailContent">Stored Email Content</a>] payload and [<a href="#IMAPEmailAttributes">IMAP Email Attributes</a>] attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [IMAP](#imap)

#### Throws

- EMAIL:EMAIL_LIST
- EMAIL:ACCESSING_FOLDER
- EMAIL:CONNECTIVITY

### Mark As Deleted

`<email:mark-as-deleted>`

Marks an incoming email as DELETED, this way the marked email(s) are scheduled for deletion when the
folder closes, this means that the email is not physically eliminated from the mailbox folder, but
itâs state changes. All DELETED marked emails are going to be eliminated from the mailbox when one
of IMAPOperations#expungeFolder(MailboxConnection, String) or
IMAPOperations#delete(MailboxConnection, String, long) is executed. This operation targets a single
email.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mailbox Folder</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mailbox folder where the emails are going to be marked as deleted</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INBOX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Email ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email ID Number of the email to mark as deleted.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [IMAP](#imap)

#### Throws

- EMAIL:EMAIL_NOT_FOUND
- EMAIL:ACCESSING_FOLDER
- EMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- EMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Mark As Read

`<email:mark-as-read>`

Marks a single email as READ changing itâs state in the specified mailbox folder. This operation can
targets a single email.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mailbox Folder</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Folder where the emails are going to be marked as read</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INBOX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Email ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email ID Number of the email to mark as read.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [IMAP](#imap)

#### Throws

- EMAIL:EMAIL_NOT_FOUND
- EMAIL:ACCESSING_FOLDER
- EMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- EMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Move To Folder

`<email:move-to-folder>`

Moves the email with ID emailId from the sourceFolder to the targetFolder.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Source Folder</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mailbox folder where the emails to be moved are located.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INBOX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Folder</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mailbox folder where the emails will be moved.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Email ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email ID Number of the email to move.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Create Target Folder</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Create the target folder if it does not exist.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [IMAP](#imap)

#### Throws

- EMAIL:EMAIL_NOT_FOUND
- EMAIL:ACCESSING_FOLDER
- EMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- EMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### Count messages - POP3

`<email:count-messages-pop3>`

Counts the emails in the INBOX_FOLDER.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [POP3](#pop3)

#### Throws

- EMAIL:EMAIL_COUNT_MESSAGES
- EMAIL:ACCESSING_FOLDER
- EMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- EMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

### List - POP3

`<email:list-pop3>`

List all the emails (with pagination) in the configured pop3 mailBoxFolder that match with the
specified pop3Matcher criteria. As the POP3 protocol does not support the capability to find
specific emails from its UID in a folder to move/delete it. a parameter deleteAfterRetrieve is
available for deleting the emails from the server right after being retrieved.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Mailbox Folder</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Mailbox folder where the emails are going to be fetched</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INBOX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Match with</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#pop3-matcher">Pop3 Matcher</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Email Matcher which gives the capability of filter the retrieved emails</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delete After Retrieve</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies if the returned emails must be deleted after being retrieved or not.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Page Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size of the page used by the PagingProvider implementation for fetching the emails from the POP3 server</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>10</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pagination Offset</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size of the pagination offset used by the PagingProvider implementation for fetching the emails from the POP3 server</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>0</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Limit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of emails retrieved by the operation. Take into account that this limit only applies to the emails effectively retrieved by the operation (the ones which matched the IMAPEmailPredicateBuilder criteria) and doesnât imply any restriction over the amount of emails being retrieved from the mailbox server.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>-1</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-iterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-iterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attachment Naming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NAME</p></li><li><p>NAME_HEADERS</p></li><li><p>NAME_HEADERS_SUBJECT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how attachment names should be retrieved.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that storesoperationâs output will be placed</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of [<a href="#StoredEmailContent">Stored Email Content</a>] payload and [<a href="#POP3EmailAttributes">POP3 Email Attributes</a>] attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [POP3](#pop3)

#### Throws

- EMAIL:EMAIL_LIST
- EMAIL:ACCESSING_FOLDER
- EMAIL:CONNECTIVITY

### Send

`<email:send>`

Sends an email message. The message is sent to all recipient toAddresses, ccAddresses, bccAddresses
specified in the message. The content of the message aims to be some type of text (text/plan,
text/html) and its composed by the body and itâs content type. If no content is specified then the
incoming payload itâs going to be converted into plain text if possible.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From Address</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The "From" sender address. The person that is going to send the messages, if not set, it defaults to the from address specified in the config.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>To Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The recipient addresses of "To" (primary) type.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cc Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The recipient addresses of "Cc" (carbon copy) type</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bcc Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The recipient addresses of "Bcc" (blind carbon copy) type</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reply To Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The email addresses to which this email should be replied.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subject</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The subject of the email.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The headers that this email carry.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Text body of the message. Aims to be text in any format</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>ContentType</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ContentType of the body text. Example: "text/plain".</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The character encoding of the body. If it is configured, it overrides the one inferred from the body.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Transfer Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding used to indicate the type of transformation that has been used to represent the body in an acceptable manner for transport. The value is case insensitive. Known encodings: * BASE64 * QUOTED-PRINTABLE * 8BIT * 7BIT * BINARY</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Base64</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attachments</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The attachments for an Email, that is sent along the email body.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Content Transfer Encoding</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encoding used to indicate the type of transformation that has been used to represent the body in an acceptable manner for transport. The value is case insensitive. Known encodings: * BASE64 * QUOTED-PRINTABLE * 8BIT * 7BIT * BINARY</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Base64</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [SMTP](#smtp)

#### Throws

- EMAIL:SEND
- EMAIL:CONNECTIVITY
- EMAIL:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Sources

### On New Email - IMAP

`<email:listener-imap>`

Retrieves all the emails from an IMAP mailbox folder, watermark can be enabled for polled items.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Folder</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the folder to poll emails from. Defaults to "INBOX".</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INBOX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delete After Retrieve</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables the deletion of the polled emails after being retrieved. This is disabled by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enable Watermark</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If watermark should be applied to the polled emails or not. Default to true.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>true</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Imap Matcher</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#imap-matcher">Imap Matcher</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A matcher to filter emails retrieved by this polling source. For default already read emails will be filtered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enable Remote Search</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If search filters should be resolved on server(true) or client(false) side. Default to false because some email servers might not be fully compliant with rfc-3501âs search terms. Activating this feature will diminish traffic by reducing the amount of emails brought to client side for processing.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scheduling Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attachment Naming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NAME</p></li><li><p>NAME_HEADERS</p></li><li><p>NAME_HEADERS_SUBJECT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how attachment names should be retrieved.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#StoredEmailContent">Stored Email Content</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#BaseEmailAttributes">Base Email Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [IMAP](#imap)

### On New Email - POP3

`<email:listener-pop3>`

Retrieves all the emails from an POP3 mailbox folder.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Folder</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the folder to poll emails from. Defaults to "INBOX".</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INBOX</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Delete After Retrieve</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables the deletion of the polled emails after being retrieved. This is disabled by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Pop3 Matcher</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#pop3-matcher">Pop3 Matcher</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A matcher to filter emails retrieved by this polling source.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scheduling Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attachment Naming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NAME</p></li><li><p>NAME_HEADERS</p></li><li><p>NAME_HEADERS_SUBJECT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates how attachment names should be retrieved.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#StoredEmailContent">Stored Email Content</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#BaseEmailAttributes">Base Email Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [POP3](#pop3)

## Types

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Pooling Profile

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Active</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Controls the maximum number of Mule components that can be borrowed from a session at one time. When set to a negative value, there is no limit to the number of components that may be active at one time. When maxActive is exceeded, the pool is said to be exhausted.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Controls the maximum number of Mule components that can sit idle in the pool at any time. When set to a negative value, there is no limit to the number of Mule components that may be idle at one time.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Wait</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the number of milliseconds to wait for a pooled component to become available when the pool is exhausted and the exhaustedAction is set to WHEN_EXHAUSTED_WAIT.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Min Eviction Millis</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines the minimum amount of time an object may sit idle in the pool before it is eligible for eviction. When non-positive, no objects will be evicted from the pool due to idle time alone.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Eviction Check Interval Millis</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the number of milliseconds between runs of the object evictor. When non-positive, no object evictor is executed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Exhausted Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_GROW</p></li><li><p>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_WAIT</p></li><li><p>WHEN_EXHAUSTED_FAIL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies the behavior of the Mule component pool when the pool is exhausted. Possible values are: "WHEN_EXHAUSTED_FAIL", which will throw a NoSuchElementException, "WHEN_EXHAUSTED_WAIT", which will block by invoking Object.wait(long) until a new or idle object is available, or WHEN_EXHAUSTED_GROW, which will create a new Mule instance and return it, essentially making maxActive meaningless. If a positive maxWait value is supplied, it will block for at most that many milliseconds, after which a NoSuchElementException will be thrown. If maxThreadWait is a negative value, it will block indefinitely.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Initialisation Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>INITIALISE_NONE</p></li><li><p>INITIALISE_ONE</p></li><li><p>INITIALISE_ALL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines how components in a pool should be initialized. The possible values are: INITIALISE_NONE (will not load any components into the pool on startup), INITIALISE_ONE (will load one initial component into the pool on startup), or INITIALISE_ALL (will load all components in the pool on startup)</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Disabled</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether pooling should be disabled</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#standard-revocation-check">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#custom-ocsp-responder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#crl-file">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations.
