Setting this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Keystore

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field (the secondary method) to check certificate validity.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Do not use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Avoid verification failure.</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>false</code></p></li></ul></div><div><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response. If specified, the alias must be in the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures an expiration policy strategy.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Stored Email Content

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Body</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attachments</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Base Email Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Number</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>To Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cc Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bcc Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reply To Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of HTTP headers in the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subject</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Received Date</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sent Date</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Imap Matcher

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Seen</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>REQUIRE</p></li><li><p>INCLUDE</p></li><li><p>EXCLUDE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates if should retrieve 'seen' or 'not seen' emails</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INCLUDE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Answered</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>REQUIRE</p></li><li><p>INCLUDE</p></li><li><p>EXCLUDE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates if should retrieve 'answered' or 'not answered' emails</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INCLUDE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Deleted</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>REQUIRE</p></li><li><p>INCLUDE</p></li><li><p>EXCLUDE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates if should retrieve 'marked as deleted' or 'not marked as deleted' emails</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INCLUDE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Recent</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>REQUIRE</p></li><li><p>INCLUDE</p></li><li><p>EXCLUDE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>"Indicates if should retrieve 'recent' or 'not recent' emails</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>INCLUDE</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Received Since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates since which date the received emails must be retrieved</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Received Until</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates until which date the received emails must be retrieved</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sent Since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates since which date the sent emails must be retrieved</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sent Until</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates until which date the sent emails must be retrieved</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subject Regex</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Subject Regex to match with the wanted emails</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From Regex</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>From Email Address Regex to match with the wanted emails</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a
redelivery policy to any source in a flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of times that a redelivered request can be processed unsuccessfully before returning a REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule uses a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secure hashing algorithm to use if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>true</code>. If the payload of the message is a Java object, Mule ignores this value and returns the value that the payloadâs <code>hashCode()</code> returned.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more expressions that determine when a message was redelivered. You can set this property only if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object Store</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the object store that stores the redelivery counter for each message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### IMAP Email Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Flags</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#EmailFlags">Email Flags</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Number</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>To Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cc Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Bcc Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reply To Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of HTTP headers in the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subject</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Received Date</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sent Date</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Email Flags

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Answered</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Deleted</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Draft</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Recent</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Seen</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>false</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The amount of instances that is initially be allowed to be kept in memory to consume the stream and provide random access to it. If the stream contains more data than can fit into this buffer, then the buffer expands according to the bufferSizeIncrement attribute, with an upper limit of maxInMemorySize. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised when the buffer gets full. Default value is 100 instances.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size of the buffer. If the buffer size exceeds this value, Mule raises a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error. A value of less than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Iterable

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Objects</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The maximum amount of instances to keep in memory. If more than that is required, content on the disk is buffered.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Pop3 Matcher

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Received Since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates since which date the received emails must be retrieved</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Received Until</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates until which date the received emails must be retrieved</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sent Since</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates since which date the sent emails must be retrieved</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sent Until</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Indicates until which date the sent emails must be retrieved</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subject Regex</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Subject Regex to match with the wanted emails</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>From Regex</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>From Email Address Regex to match with the wanted emails</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### POP3 Email Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Addresses</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of HTTP headers in the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subject</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Received ---

# Email Connector Examples 1.6

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To fully benefit from using Anypoint Connector for Email (Email Connector), see the following
examples to configure the connector.

- [Send Emails and Attachments](email-send)  
  Configure the **Send** operation to send emails and attachments over SMTP or SMTPS servers.
- [List Emails](email-list)  
  Configure Email Connector and IMAP or POP3 protocols to list emails.
- [Connect to Gmail](email-gmail)  
  Configure Email Connector and IMAP, POP3, and SMTP protocols to connect to your Gmail account.
- [Trigger a Flow When Receiving a New Email](email-trigger)  
  Configure the **On New Email - IMAP** or **On New Email - POP3** sources to retrieve all the
  emails in an IMAP or POP3 mailbox, and generate a new message for each unread email found.
  Additionally, configure the **Delete After Retrieve**, **Enable Watermark**, **IMAP Matcher** or
  **POP3 Matcher**, **Attachment Naming Strategy**, and **Enable Remote Search** parameters for
  these sources.
- [Set Parsing Text File Attachment as Body Message](email-attachment)  
  Set the system property `parsing.text.attachment.as.body` to change the behavior of parsing
  attached text files when the file is the only content in the message.
- [Move Messages Between Folders for IMAP/S Connection Providers](email-move-folder)  
  Configure the **Move to folder** operation to move messages between folders for IMAP and IMAPS
  connection providers.
- [Count messages with Email Connector](email-connector-count-messages)  
  Configure the **Count messages - IMAP** and **Count messages - POP3** operations get the total
  amount of messages in a specified mailbox folder.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Connect to Gmail with Email Connector Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To connect Anypoint Connector for Email (Email Connector) to your Gmail account, enable **Less
Secure Apps** in your account and configure a global element for the server connection IMAPS, POP3S,
or SMTPS you want to use.

## Configure an App Password in Your Gmail Account

To send emails to a Gmail account via Email Connector, you must
[configure an app password](https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/185833?visit_id=637917932546436561-2352829986&p=InvalidSecondFactor&rd=1)
in your Gmail account.

Use the app password instead of your regular Gmail email password, which enables you to use email
protected by MFA (Multi-Factor Authentication).

## Configure an IMAPS Connection

To connect Email Connector to your Gmail account using an IMAPS connection, configure a global
element:

1.  In Studio, navigate to the **Global Elements** view.
2.  Click **Create** to open the **Choose Global Type** view.
3.  In **Filter**, type `email`, select **Email IMAP**, and click **OK**.
4.  In the **Email IMAP** window, for the **Connection** field, select **IMAPS Connection**.
5.  In the **General** section, enter the following values:
    - **Host** `imap.gmail.com`
    - **Port** `993`
    - **User** `user@gmail.com`
    - **Password** `Gmailpassword`

6.  In the **Trust Store Configuration** section, select the **Insecure** checkbox.
7.  Click **OK**.

![IMAPs Configuration to Gmail](_images/email-gmail-imap.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the configuration looks like this:

```xml
<email:imap-config name="Email_IMAP">
  <email:imaps-connection host="imap.gmail.com"
    port="995" user="user@gmail.com"
    password="mypassword">
    <tls:context>
        <tls:trust-store insecure="true"/>
    </tls:context>
  </email:imaps-connection>
</email:imap-config>
```

## Configure a POP3S Connection

To connect Email Connector to your Gmail account using a POP3S connection, configure a global
element:

1.  In Studio, navigate to the **Global Elements** view.
2.  Click **Create** to open the **Choose Global Type** view.
3.  In **Filter**, type `email`, select **Email POP3**, and click **OK**.
4.  In the **Email POP3** window, for the **Connection** field, select **POP3S Connection**.
5.  In the **General** section, enter the following values:
    - **Host** `pop.gmail.com`
    - **Port** `995`
    - **User** `user@gmail.com`
    - **Password** `Gmailpassword`

6.  In the **Trust Store Configuration** section, select the **Insecure** checkbox.
7.  Click **OK**.

![POP3S Configuration to Gmail](_images/email-gmail-pop3.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the configuration looks like this:

```xml
<email:pop3-config name="Email_POP3">
    <email:pop3s-connection host="pop.gmail.com"
      port="995"  user="user@gmail.com"
      password="Gmailpassword">
        <tls:context>
            <tls:trust-store insecure="true"/>
        </tls:context>
    </email:pop3s-connection>
</email:pop3-config>
```

## Configure an SMTP Connection

To connect Email Connector to your Gmail account using an SMTPS connection, configure a global
element:

1.  In Studio, navigate to the **Global Elements** view.
2.  Click **Create** to open the **Choose Global Type** view.
3.  In **Filter**, type `email`, select **Email SMTP**, and click **OK**.
4.  In the **Email SMTP** window, for the **Connection** field, select **SMTPS Connection**.
5.  In the **General** section, enter the following values:
    - **Host** `smtp.gmail.com`
    - **Port** `465`
    - **User** `user@gmail.com`
    - **Password** `Gmailpassword`

6.  In the **Trust Store Configuration** section, select the **Insecure** checkbox.
7.  Click **OK**.

![SMTPS Configuration to Gmail](_images/email-gmail-smtp.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the configuration looks like this:

```xml
<email:smtp-config name="Email_SMTP" from="sender@gmail.com">
    <email:smtps-connection host="smtp.gmail.com"
      port="465" password="Gmailpassword"
      user="user@gmail.com">
        <tls:context enabledProtocols="TLSv1.2,SSLv3">
            <tls:trust-store insecure="true"/>
        </tls:context>
    </email:smtps-connection>
</email:smtp-config>
```

## Troubleshoot SMTPS Connection Issues

The method that you use to troubleshoot SMTPS connection issues depends on whether or not your Gmail
account uses two-factor authentication:

- If your account uses two-factor authentication, generate an app-specific password and use that
  instead of your normal password.

  See [Sign in Using App Password](https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/185833) for details.
  You do not need to enable **Less Secure Apps** in your Gmail account.

- If your Gmail account does not use two-factor authentication, set up and enable **Less Secure
  Apps** in your Gmail account, and if your password does not work, go to
  [Allow Access to Your Google Account](https://accounts.google.com/DisplayUnlockCaptcha) and follow
  these steps:
  1.  Enter your username and password.
  2.  Enter the letters on the captcha screen.
  3.  Return to your Mule app and rerun the flow.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Email Connector Examples](email-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# List Emails with Email Connector Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Email (Email Connector) provides the **List - IMAP** and **List - POP3**
operations, which list emails from any part of the flow. The following examples illustrate how to
configure these operations for the connector:

- [List Emails from an IMAPS Server](#list-emails-from-an-imaps-server)  
  Configure the **List - IMAP** operation to list all the emails in the configured IMAP mailbox
  folder from an IMAPS server that has a TLS connection. Additionally, set a **For Each** scope
  component and two **Set Variable** components to iterate over the emails and save the email body
  and attachment content in variables.
- [List Emails from a POP3 Server](#list-emails-from-a-pop3-server)  
  Configure the **List - POP3** operation to list all the emails in the configured POP3 mailbox
  folder that matched the criteria specified in the **Match with** parameter.

These example use variables for some field values. You can either:

- Replace the variables with their values in the code.
- Provide the values for each variable in a properties file and then refer to that file from the
  connector configuration.  
  If you donât know how to use a properties file, see
  [Configuring Property Placeholders](../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-app-properties-to-configure).

## List Emails from an IMAPS Server

The following example illustrates how to list all the emails in the configured IMAP mailbox folder
from an IMAPS server that has a TLS connection. A **Scheduler** component initiates the flow, and
then the **List - IMAP** operation lists the emails. Subsequently, a **For Each** scope component
and two **Set Variable** components iterate over the emails, and save the body and attachments of
the email content payload in variables:

The following screenshot shows the app flow for this example:

![List Emails from an IMAPS Server flow](_images/email-list-flow-1.png)

Figure 1. List Emails from an IMAPS Server flow

To create the flow:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select the **Scheduler** component and drag it onto the canvas.  
    This source initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met.
3.  Drag the **List - IMAP** operation to the right of the **Scheduler** source.
4.  On the **List - IMAP** configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector
    configuration** field to configure a global element for the operation.
5.  For **Connection**, select **IMAPS Connection**.
6.  In the **General** tab, enter the following values:
    - **Host** `imap.gmail.com`
    - **Port** `993`
    - **User** `user1@domain.com`
    - **Password** `passwordconnection`

7.  In the **TLS Configuration** section, select **Edit inline**.
8.  In the **Trust Store Configuration** section, enter the following values:
    - **Path** `aTruststore.jks`
    - **Password** `passwordtrust`

9.  In the **Key Store Configuration** section, enter the following values:
    - **Path** `aKeystore`
    - **Password** `passwordkey`

10. In the **Advanced** section, set **Enabled Protocols** to `TLSv1.2,SSLv3`.
11. Click **OK**.

![IMAPS Connection Configuration](_images/email-list-imap-config.png)

12. On the **List - IMAP** configuration screen, set the **Mailbox folder** field to `INBOX`.

![List - IMAP Configuration](_images/email-list-listimap-config.png)

13. Drag the **For each** scope component to the right of the **List - IMAP** operation.
14. Drag a **Set Variable** component inside the **For each** scope component.
15. Set **Name** to `email-content` and **Value** to `payload.body`.  
    This component saves the body content of the email in a new variable.
16. Drag another **Set Variable** component to the right of the first **Set Variable** component.
17. Set **Name** to `json-attachment` and **Value** to `#[payload.attachments.'attachment-name']`.  
    This component saves the email `.json` attachment to a new variable.
18. Save and run the app.

### XML for Listing Emails from an IMAPS Server

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:tls="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls" xmlns:email="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email/current/mule-email.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls/current/mule-tls.xsd">
	<email:imap-config name="Email_IMAP" doc:name="Email IMAP" doc:id="e1aa73b1-4719-4463-b0d9-4b92fb6b2622" >
		<email:imaps-connection host="imap.gmail.com" user="user1@domain.com" password="passwordconnection" >
			<tls:context enabledProtocols="TLSv1.2,SSLv3">
				<tls:trust-store path="aTruststore.jks" password="passwordtrust" />
				<tls:key-store path="aKeystore" password="passwordkey" />
			</tls:context>
		</email:imaps-connection>
	</email:imap-config>
	<flow name="ListIMAP" >
		<scheduler doc:name="Scheduler" >
			<scheduling-strategy >
				<fixed-frequency />
			</scheduling-strategy>
		</scheduler>
		<email:list-imap doc:name="List - IMAP" config-ref="Email_IMAP"/>
		<foreach doc:name="For Each" >
			<set-variable value="#[payload.body]" doc:name="Set Variable" variableName="email-content"/>
			<set-variable value="#[payload.attachments.'attachment-name']" doc:name="Set Variable" variableName="json-attachment"/>
		</foreach>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Set Pagination Offset for List IMAP

The **Pagination offset** field enables you to set the pagination offset and retrieve a certain
record (or subset of records) within a particular page for the **List - IMAP** operation.

1.  In Studio, select the **List - IMAP** operation from your flow.
2.  Set **Connector configuration** to **Email_IMAP_outlook**.
3.  Set **Page size** to `10`.
4.  Set **Pagination offset** to `2`.

![List Imap operation with Pagination offset field configured](_images/email-pagination-imap.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the `paginationOffset` configuration looks like this:

```xml
    <email:list-imap doc:name="List - IMAP"
                          config-ref="Email_IMAP"
                          pageSize="10"
                          paginationOffset="2"
    />
```

## List Emails from a POP3 Server

When listing emails, you can filter them using the **Match with** parameter, which defines the
criteria to process the emails. All matcher attributes are optional. They are also related to each
other under an `AND` operator, meaning that all the criteria must be true. These attributes vary
depending on the type of configuration that you are using. For example, the IMAP protocol provides
more metadata about the retrieved email such as the `recent`, `seen`, `deleted`, and `answered`
attribute flags.

The following example illustrates how to list all the emails in the configured POP3 mailbox folder
that match the specified criteria set in the **Match with** parameter for the configuration. A
**Scheduler** component initiates the flow, and then the **List - POP3** operation lists only the
emails whose subject regex match `BETA`:

The following screenshot shows the app flow for this example:

![List Emails from an POP3 Server flow](_images/email-list-flow-2.png)

Figure 2. List Emails from an POP3 Server flow

To create the flow:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select the **Scheduler** component and drag it onto the canvas.  
    This source initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met.
3.  Drag the **List - POP3** operation to the right of the **Scheduler** source.
4.  On the **List - POP3** configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector
    configuration** field to configure a global element for the operation.
5.  For **Connection**, select **POP3 Connection**.
6.  In the **General** tab, enter the following values:
    - **Host** `pop.gmail.com`
    - **Port** `995`
    - **User** `user2@domain.com`
    - **Password** `passwordconnection`

7.  Click **OK**.

![POP3 Connection Configuration](_images/email-list-pop3-config.png)

8.  On the **List - POP3** configuration screen, set the **Mailbox folder** field to `INBOX`.
9.  Set **Match with** to `Edit inline`
10. Set **Subject regex** to `BETA.`

![List - IMAP Configuration](_images/email-list-listpop3-config.png)

11. Save and run the app.

### XML for Listing Emails from a POP3 server

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:tls="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls" xmlns:email="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email/current/mule-email.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls/current/mule-tls.xsd">
	<email:pop3-config name="Email_POP3" doc:name="Email POP3" >
		<email:pop3-connection host="pop.gmail.com" port="995" user="user2@domain.com" password="passwordconnection" />
	</email:pop3-config>
	<flow name="ListPOP3" >
		<scheduler doc:name="Scheduler">
			<scheduling-strategy >
				<fixed-frequency />
			</scheduling-strategy>
		</scheduler>
		<email:list-pop3 doc:name="List - POP3" config-ref="Email_POP3">
			<email:pop3-matcher subjectRegex="BETA" />
		</email:list-pop3>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Set Pagination Offset for List POP3

The **Pagination offset** field enables you to set the pagination offset and retrieve a certain
record (or subset of records) within a particular page for the **List - POP3** operation.

1.  In Studio, select the **List - POP3** operation from your flow.
2.  Set **Connector configuration** to **Email_POP3**.
3.  Set **Page size** to `10`.
4.  Set **Pagination offset** to `2`.

![List POP3 operation with Pagination offset field configured](_images/email-pagination-pop3.png)

In the **Configuration XML** editor, the `paginationOffset` configuration looks like this:

```xml
    <email:list-pop3 doc:name="List - POP3"
                          config-ref="Email_POP3"
                          pageSize="10"
                          paginationOffset="2"
    />
```

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Email Connector Examples](email-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)

# Move Messages Between Folders for IMAP/S Connection Providers Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Email (Email Connector) provides the **Move to folder** operation that
enables you to move messages to folders for IMAP and IMAPS connection providers. The operationâs
main features include:

- Moving messages between folders of the email server.
- Preserving the moved message UID.
- Creating the target folder if the folder does not exist or fails depending on a parameter.

The following examples illustrate how to configure the operation for the connector.

## Move a Message from One Folder to Another Folder

The following example illustrates how to move a received message from one folder to another folder.
An **On New Email - IMAP** source initiates the flow, and then the **Mark as read** operation reads
the received email in the `INBOX` folder. Subsequently, the **Move to folder** operation moves the
email to the `TARGET` folder:

The following screenshot shows the app flow for this example:

![Move a Message from One Folder to Another Folder flow](_images/email-movetofolder-flow-1.png)

To create the flow:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, drag the **On New Email - IMAP** operation onto the canvas.
3.  On the **On New Email - IMAP** configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to configure a global element for the operation.
4.  Set **Connection** to **IMAPS Connection**.
5.  Complete the following fields:
    - **Host**: `test.host.com`
    - **Port**: `993`
    - **User**: `test123@testuser.com`
    - **Password**: `testpassword`

6.  Click **OK**.
7.  Set **Folder** to `INBOX`.
8.  Drag a **Mark as read** operation to the right of the **On New Email - IMAP** source.
9.  For **Connector configuration**, select the previously created global configuration.
10. Set **Mailbox folder** to `INBOX`.
11. Set **Email ID** to the expression `attributes.id`.
12. Drag a **Move to folder** operation to the right of the **Mark as read** operation.
13. For **Connector configuration**, select the previously created global configuration.
14. Set **Mailbox folder** to `INBOX`.
15. Set **Target folder** to `TEST`.
16. Set **Email ID** to the expression `attributes.id`.
17. Save and run your Mule application.
18. Send a new email to the email address.

### XML for Moving a Message from One Folder to Another Folder

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:tls="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls"
	xmlns:email="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls/current/mule-tls.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email/current/mule-email.xsd">
	<email:imap-config name="Email_IMAP_outlook" doc:name="Email IMAP" >
		<email:imaps-connection
                host="test.host.com"
                user="test123@testuser.com"
                password="testpassword">
			<tls:context >
				<tls:trust-store insecure="true" />
			</tls:context>
		</email:imaps-connection>
	</email:imap-config>
	<flow name="email-oldFlow1" >
		<email:listener-imap doc:name="On New Email - IMAP" config-ref="Email_IMAP_outlook">
			<scheduling-strategy >
				<fixed-frequency />
			</scheduling-strategy>
		</email:listener-imap>
		<email:mark-as-read emailId="#[attributes.id]" doc:name="Mark as read" config-ref="Email_IMAP_outlook"/>
		<email:move-to-folder emailId="#[attributes.id]" doc:name="Move imap" config-ref="Email_IMAP_outlook" targetFolder="TEST"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Move a Message to a Target Folder and Create the Folder When Is Non-existent

The following example illustrates how to move a received message from one folder to another, if the
target folder does not exist, the folder is then created. An **On New Email - IMAP** source
initiates the flow, and then the **Mark as read** operation reads the received email in the `INBOX`
folder. Subsequently, the **Move to folder** operation moves the email to the `TARGET` folder. If
that folder does not exist, it is created automatically.

The following screenshot shows the app flow for this example:

![Move a Message to a Target Folder and Create the Folder When Is Non-existent flow](_images/email-movetofolder-flow-1.png)

To create the flow:

1.  Create a new Mule project in Studio.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, drag the **On New Email - IMAP** operation onto the canvas.
3.  On the **On New Email - IMAP** configuration screen, click the plus sign (**+**) next to the
    **Connector configuration** field to configure a global element for the operation.
4.  Set **Connection** to **IMAPS Connection**.
5.  Complete the following fields:
    - **Host**: `test.host.com`
    - **Port**: `993`
    - **User**: `test123@testuser.com`
    - **Password**: `testpassword`

6.  Click **OK**.
7.  Set **Folder** to **INBOX**.
8.  Drag a **Mark as read** operation to the right of the **On New Email - IMAP** source.
9.  For **Connector configuration**, select the previously created global configuration.
10. Set **Mailbox folder** to `INBOX`.
11. Set **Email ID** to the expression `attributes.id`.
12. Drag a **Move to folder** operation to the right of the **Mark as read** operation.
13. For **Connector configuration**, select the previously created global configuration.
14. Set **Mailbox folder** to `INBOX`.
15. Set **Target folder** to `TEST_B`.
16. Set **Email ID** to the expression `attributes.id`.
17. Set **Create target folder** to **TRUE**.

![Create Folder When Is Non-existent](_images/email-movetofolder-create.png)

18. Save and run your Mule application.
19. Send a new email to the email address.

### XML for Moving a Message to a Target Folder and Create the Folder When Is Non-existent

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:tls="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls"
	xmlns:email="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls/current/mule-tls.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email/current/mule-email.xsd">
	<email:imap-config name="Email_IMAP_outlook" doc:name="Email IMAP" >
		<email:imaps-connection
                host="test.host.com"
                user="test123@testuser.com"
                password="testpassword">
			<tls:context >
				<tls:trust-store insecure="true" />
			</tls:context>
		</email:imaps-connection>
	</email:imap-config>
	<flow name="email-oldFlow1" >
		<email:listener-imap doc:name="On New Email - IMAP" config-ref="Email_IMAP_outlook">
			<scheduling-strategy >
				<fixed-frequency />
			</scheduling-strategy>
		</email:listener-imap>
		<email:mark-as-read emailId="#[attributes.id]" doc:name="Mark as read"  config-ref="Email_IMAP_outlook"/>
		<email:move-to-folder emailId="#[attributes.id]" doc:name="Move imap"  config-ref="Email_IMAP_outlook" targetFolder="TEST_B" createTargetFolder="true"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Email Connector Examples](email-examples)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
